summary: openai-agents-helpers exfiltrates developer identity via postinstall to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads the developer's identity files (~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml) to extract git email, GitHub login, and GitHub email, then collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, cwd, CI provider, and env vars (GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL, EMAIL). The data is assembled into a JSON body `{ identity: { hostname, username, scmEmail, githubLogin, githubEmail }, context: { cwd, ci } }` and POSTed via https.request to `npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app`. A header comment falsely claims the data is 'fully anonymous' and 'one-way hashed', but no hashing occurs — raw PII is transmitted. The package additionally impersonates OpenAI's official @openai/agents SDK (name `openai-agents-helpers`, author `OpenAI Agents JS Guide <hello@openai-agents-js.guide>`, homepage `openai-agents-js.guide`) and declares `main: dist/index.js`, a file that is absent from the tarball — confirming the package has no real library functionality and exists solely to execute the postinstall exfiltration payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
